summary: The artifacts establish a clean, consistent top-level boundary: Postgres `run_events` is authoritative; runs/generations/agenomes/candidates/reviews/checks/fitness/lineage tables, dashboard, SSE, Langfuse, and Neo4j are all explicitly DERIVED and never authoritative. This is stated repeatedly and coherently across DOMAIN_MODEL §"Source Of Truth"/Invariants, DATA_MODEL §Purpose/Core Tables, ARCHITECTURE §4/§7, and THREAT_MODEL trust boundaries — no contradictions with the locked baseline. The scoring-policy-version question is handled well (authoritative, pinned per run, agents emit evidence only). However, two load-bearing edge cases are dangerously underspecified and one is internally contradictory. The critical one: novelty EMBEDDINGS are classified as a derived projection (the `embeddings` table is "rebuildable"), yet replay forbids embedding-provider calls and the `novelty.scored` event does NOT persist the vector — so a derived/rebuildable artifact is actually irreproducible without a forbidden provider call. Whether embeddings are authoritative-once-computed and where raw vs normalized model outputs physically live are both ambiguous and must be nailed down before the persistence/scoring tracks build.

findings.finding: Novelty embeddings are classified as a DERIVED/rebuildable projection, but this contradicts the replay rule. DATA_MODEL Core Tables lists `embeddings` as a projection table ('Novelty vectors... if pgvector is used') and ARCHITECTURE §7 lists `embeddings` under 'Required projections'. But DATA_MODEL Replay Rules forbids replay from 'call[ing] ... embedding providers', and the `novelty.scored` event persists only 'comparison set, method, score, explanation' — NOT the embedding vectors. A derived read model is by definition rebuildable from the event log; embeddings are not, because the vector is never in an event and cannot be recomputed without the forbidden provider call. So embeddings are de facto authoritative-once-computed source data masquerading as a derived projection.
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md Core Tables (line 72) + Replay Rules (lines 152-153) + Required Event Types `novelty.scored` (line 50); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §7 Required projections (line 283)
findings.proposed_fix: Reclassify embedding vectors as authoritative input data, not a rebuildable projection. Persist the actual vector (and the embedding model id + dimension) either inside the `novelty.scored` event payload or in a dedicated append-only/immutable `embeddings` store keyed by candidateId, written at scoring time. State explicitly that the `embeddings` store is authoritative-derived (computed once, then immutable and replay-readable) — distinct from the rebuildable dashboard/lineage projections. Add an invariant: replay reads stored vectors and recomputes only the cosine/comparison math (which is deterministic and allowed), never re-embeds.

findings.finding: Raw vs normalized model outputs are persisted only as 'refs', and the storage location/ownership of the referenced content is undefined. The `candidate.created` event must persist 'raw/normalized output refs' (a reference), and CandidateIdea/CriticReview/CheckResult carry 'evidence refs'/'evidenceRefs' (EvidenceRef[]), but no artifact says where the referenced raw and normalized blobs actually live or which store owns them authoritatively. Replay 'uses persisted events and stored outputs' (DOMAIN_MODEL invariant), so the 'stored outputs' behind those refs are load-bearing for replay, yet their source of truth is unspecified.
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md `candidate.created` row (line 47), CandidateIdea.evidenceRefs (line 87), EvidenceRef referenced but undefined; DOMAIN_MODEL.md Invariants 'Replay uses persisted events and stored outputs' (line 227)
findings.proposed_fix: Decide and document: store both raw provider output and normalized candidate output inline in the authoritative event payload (recommended for MVP — JSONB is already the chosen payload format), so a ref is just a pointer within the event log and no external blob store is authoritative. Define the EvidenceRef shape (e.g. {kind, eventId|table, locator}) and state that every ref resolves to data already inside the authoritative Postgres tier, never to an external/non-authoritative store, so replay never dereferences something it cannot reproduce.

findings.finding: NoveltyScore source-of-truth is stated as an either/or ('Event log or fitness component'), leaving its authoritative home ambiguous. Fitness depends on novelty as a component, and all selection decisions must be explainable from persisted event data, so an ambiguous owner for the novelty signal undermines auditability. The `novelty.scored` event exists in DATA_MODEL's required event types, which implies the event log IS the source of truth — but DOMAIN_MODEL leaves it open.
findings.evidence: DOMAIN_MODEL.md Core Entities NoveltyScore row 'Source Of Truth: Event log or fitness component' (line 117); DATA_MODEL.md `novelty.scored` required event (line 50); DOMAIN_MODEL.md business rule 'All selection decisions must be explainable from persisted event data' (line 197)
findings.proposed_fix: Lock NoveltyScore source of truth to the `novelty.scored` event (consistent with DATA_MODEL's required event list). The `fitness.scored` event then references the novelty score it consumed by event id / value rather than re-deriving it. Remove the 'or fitness component' alternative from the DOMAIN_MODEL ownership column.

findings.finding: The `dashboard_snapshots` table is labeled 'Optional replay/read optimization ... Derived and rebuildable', but there is no stated invalidation/version trigger tying a snapshot to the event `sequence` it was built through. The LineageGraphProjection correctly carries `sequenceThrough`; dashboard_snapshots has no analogous staleness marker, risking a stale-but-trusted cached read.
findings.proposed_fix: Require any cached projection (including dashboard_snapshots) to record the `(runId, sequence)` watermark it was built through and to be discarded/rebuilt when newer events exist. This makes 'rebuildable' enforceable and prevents the THREAT_MODEL T-008 stale-display risk from leaking into cached snapshots, not just live SSE.

findings.finding: If pgvector is NOT used day one (app-level cosine path is taken), it is unspecified where embedding vectors are persisted for replay. DATA_MODEL conditions the `embeddings` table on 'if pgvector is used' and the open question explicitly leaves the pgvector-vs-app-cosine choice undecided — but the replay requirement (no re-embedding) applies in BOTH paths. With no embeddings table and no vector in the event, the app-cosine path currently has nowhere authoritative to store the vector.
findings.evidence: DATA_MODEL.md Core Tables 'if pgvector is used' (line 72) + Data Questions Remaining (line 161); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §9 Embeddings 'app-level cosine is acceptable for MVP scale' (line 363)
findings.proposed_fix: Decision needed: where do embedding vectors live when pgvector is deferred? Recommended default: persist the vector inside the `novelty.scored` event payload (JSONB float array) regardless of pgvector. pgvector then becomes purely an indexing/query optimization layered on top of the authoritative event-stored vectors, not the system of record — keeping replay vector-complete in both day-one paths.
